plan_adherence_notes: All 10 plan steps were substantially completed across 2 child tasks (uy1-backend-pnl, uy1-frontend-analytics). Backend: compute_orders_pnl/compute_market_pnl helpers implemented, both P&L endpoints (GET /v1/markets/{marketId}/accounts/{accountId}/pnl and GET /v1/accounts/{accountId}/pnl) registered and functional. Frontend: recharts installed, useMarketPnl/useAccountPnl/useMarketAnalytics hooks created, MarketAnalytics (with PriceHistoryChart and VolumeChart), PnLSummary, and AccountPnL components built and integrated into MarketDetail and Portfolio routes respectively. Minor deviation: step 9 called for integration tests using resolved market m3 as a test case for realized P&L, but the tests implemented are unit tests with mocked data rather than true integration tests against the backend. All 76 tests pass across 13 test files.
missed_questions: Access control: can account A view account B's P&L? The endpoints accept arbitrary accountId with no auth check.
Performance: O(n) order aggregation per P&L request could degrade with large order histories — no caching or pagination considered.
P&L display formatting: currency/unit conventions for displaying dollar amounts in the frontend.
reason: Implementation faithfully follows the plan and research. All core components are in place: backend P&L computation from order aggregation, two REST endpoints, recharts-based charts (price history, volume), P&L display components, and React Query hooks with polling. Components are correctly integrated into existing MarketDetail and Portfolio pages (not a new route), matching the d1 design decision. Tests cover the new analytics components and pass cleanly. The missed questions (auth, perf, formatting) are secondary concerns appropriate for follow-up work, not blockers for the analytics dashboard MVP.
